title: Oyster Reef Recovery Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Oyster Reef Recovery Act of 2025 creates a new government program to help save oyster reefs in America's waters. The Department of Commerce will run this program and give money and help to state and local governments, schools, nonprofit groups, businesses, and individuals who want to work on oyster reef projects. These projects can include fixing damaged reefs, watching over healthy reefs, and training people for jobs related to oyster reefs. Congress plans to spend fifteen million dollars each year from 2026 to 2030 to pay for this program.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Oyster Reef Recovery Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Oyster Reef Restoration and Conservation Program
sections.text:
The Administrator (which means the Secretary of Commerce working through the head of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration) must create a program called the Oyster Reef Restoration and Conservation Program. This program will help save and restore oyster reefs.

The Administrator must help covered entities (which includes federal, state, local, and tribal governments, schools and universities, nonprofit groups, shellfish businesses, and private people or companies) by giving them technical help and money. This help will be used to find the best places to restore oyster reefs, including existing reefs and places where important reefs used to be. The program will check how healthy oyster reefs are and what dangers they face by making maps and testing water quality. It will also figure out ways to protect the reefs from these dangers.

The program will carry out voluntary projects to plan, build, check on, protect, watch over, restore, and improve oyster reefs. It will work to keep existing and restored reefs healthy by using management methods that can change as needed based on the best science available. The program will help build partnerships and teach people how to take care of oyster reefs. It will create ways to monitor reefs to make sure conservation and restoration work is successful.

The program will help covered entities work together and share information about the best ways to conserve, restore, and improve oyster reefs. It will also support job training focused on coastal resilience (helping coasts survive storms and other challenges) and restoration methods, especially to help underserved communities.

The Administrator must create a competitive grant program that gives money to people who apply for it. These grants can be used for research, planning, building, checking on reefs, management, building skills, monitoring, working together and sharing information, or job training related to conserving, restoring, improving, or managing oyster reefs.

To get a grant, a person must send the Administrator an application in the form and at the time the Administrator decides. The application must include enough information to show that the project will not unreasonably interfere with commercial or recreational fishing or other water activities in that area.

Nothing in this section stops states or Indian tribes from managing oyster reefs or oyster species in their own areas.

Congress approves spending fifteen million dollars each year from two thousand twenty six through two thousand thirty to pay for this program.

The definitions section explains that Administrator means the Secretary of Commerce working through the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Covered entity means federal, state, local, or tribal government units, nongovernmental institutions, nonprofit organizations, colleges and universities, the shellfish industry, and private individuals or companies. Nonprofit organization means a group that qualifies for tax exempt status under section five oh one c three of the tax code. Program means the Oyster Reef Restoration and Conservation Program created by this law. Shellfish industry means people who grow shellfish and people who harvest shellfish.
